{
 "cells": [
  {
   "cell_type": "markdown",
   "id": "74800442",
   "metadata": {},
   "source": [
    "# Conformal Inference\n",
    "\n",
    "https://matheusfacure.github.io/python-causality-handbook/Conformal-Inference-for-Synthetic-Control.html\n",
    "\n",
    "https://arxiv.org/pdf/1712.09089.pdf"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": 1,
   "id": "b8385e86",
   "metadata": {},
   "outputs": [],
   "source": [
    "import sys\n",
    "sys.path.insert(0, '..')\n",
    "from panel_exp.panel_data import long_df_to_paneldataset, PanelDataset, TimePeriod\n",
    "from panel_exp.design import CompleteRandomization, ThinningDesign, Rerandomization, QuickBlock\n",
    "from panel_exp.design.design_metrics import imbalance\n",
    "\n",
    "from sklearn.tree import DecisionTreeRegressor\n",
    "from sklearn.linear_model import RidgeCV, Ridge\n",
    "from panel_exp.methods.scm import SyntheticControl, AugSynth \n",
    "\n",
    "from panel_exp.inference import conformal\n",
    "import pandas as pd\n",
    "from panel_exp.methods.tbr import TBR, TBRRidge\n",
    "import numpy as np\n",
    "\n",
    "from panel_exp.inference.unit_jackknife import unit_jk, unit_jackknife, time_jackknife_plus"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": 2,
   "id": "8b2c974e",
   "metadata": {},
   "outputs": [],
   "source": [
    "long_df = pd.read_csv('smoking.csv')\n",
    "long_df.columns = ['unnamed', 'unit', 'time_unit', 'y',  'lnincome','beer','age15to24','retprice']\n",
    "panel = long_df_to_paneldataset(long_df, \"time_unit\", \"unit\", \"y\", [\"California\" ], [1988 ]) \n"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": 3,
   "id": "d5954c5a",
   "metadata": {},
   "outputs": [],
   "source": [
    "pre_t_time = panel.treated_start_idxs[0]-1\n",
    "n_treated_units = len(panel.treated_units)\n",
    "n_treated_time_periods = (panel.treated_end_idxs[0] - panel.treated_start_idxs[0])\n"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": 4,
   "id": "e0a3060a",
   "metadata": {},
   "outputs": [
    {
     "name": "stdout",
     "output_type": "stream",
     "text": [
      "(30, 18)\n"
     ]
    }
   ],
   "source": [
    "print((panel.treated_end_idxs[0] , panel.treated_start_idxs[0]))"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": 5,
   "id": "0e778c69",
   "metadata": {},
   "outputs": [
    {
     "name": "stdout",
     "output_type": "stream",
     "text": [
      "12\n",
      "2000 1988\n"
     ]
    }
   ],
   "source": [
    "print((panel.treated_end_idxs[0] - panel.treated_start_idxs[0]))\n",
    "\n",
    "print(panel.times[panel.treated_end_idxs[0]] , panel.times[panel.treated_start_idxs[0]] )"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": 6,
   "id": "07436f24",

  {

   "id": "47e8a167",
   "metadata": {},
   "source": [
    "# Issue One\n",
    "\n",
    "I auto generate potential null hypothesis to test against (line 126 of impact ABC). The 1-alpha CI might get cut off if this is not a good estimate, so we'll need to update to pass a custom list. \n",
    "\n",
    "Choosing them is not easy\n",
    "\n",
    "## AugSynth Below"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": 11,
   "id": "fe11f6f6",
   "metadata": {},
   "outputs": [],
   "source": [
    "long_df = pd.read_csv('kansas_parsed.csv')\n",
    "panel_data = long_df_to_paneldataset(long_df, \"year_qtr\", \"state\", \"lngdp\", [\"Kansas\" ], [2012])\n"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": 15,
   "id": "97d6871e",
   "metadata": {},
   "outputs": [],
   "source": [
    "asynth = AugSynth(inference='Conformal', outcome_model=RidgeCV, alphas=[1e-3, 1e-2, 1e-1, 1])"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": 16,
   "id": "0182f86b",
   "metadata": {},
   "outputs": [
    {
     "data": {
      "image/png": "\n",
      "text/plain": [
       "<Figure size 1080x864 with 3 Axes>"
      ]
     },
     "metadata": {
      "needs_background": "light"
     },
     "output_type": "display_data"
    }
   ],
   "source": [
    "asynth.run_analysis(pds, outcome_model=RidgeCV, alphas=[1e-3, 1e-2, 1e-1, 1])\n",
    "\n",
    "asynth.plot()"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": 11,
   "id": "8f3218d3",
   "metadata": {},
   "outputs": [],
   "source": [
    "from panel_exp.methods.tbr import TBR, TBRRidge\n"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": 17,
   "id": "9bb8d38c",
   "metadata": {},
   "outputs": [
    {
     "name": "stdout",
     "output_type": "stream",
     "text": [
      "\n",
      "        Panel Dataset Summary\n",
      "        ---------------------\n",
      "        Number of time points: 105\n",
      "        Number of units: 50\n",
      "        Number of treated units: 1\n",
      "        Treated units: ['Kansas']\n",
      "        Treated periods: [TimePeriod(start=2012, end=None)]\n",
      "        \n"
     ]
    }
   ],
   "source": [
    "long_df = pd.read_csv('kansas_parsed.csv')\n",
    "long_df.head()\n",
    "\n",
    "long_df.columns = ['un', 'value', 'treated', 'fips', 'time_unit', 'unit']\n",
    "\n",
    "panel_data = long_df_to_paneldataset(long_df, \"time_unit\", \"unit\", \"value\", [\"Kansas\"], 2012)\n",
    "\n",
    "print(panel_data.summarize())"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": 18,
   "id": "90ac24cc",
   "metadata": {},
   "outputs": [],
   "source": [
    "wide = pd.pivot_table(long_df, columns='time_unit', index='unit', values = 'value', aggfunc=sum, fill_value=0)\n",
    "treated = ['Kansas']\n",
    "control_units = pd.DataFrame(wide.loc[[c for c in wide.index if c not in treated]].mean(axis=0), columns=['controls']).T\n",
    "treated_units = wide.loc[treated]\n",
    "\n",
    "wide_agg = pd.concat([treated_units, control_units])\n",
    "panel_data = PanelDataset(wide_agg, treated_units = treated, treated_periods=[TimePeriod(start=2012)])\n"
   ]
  },

   "cell_type": "code",

   "id": "b3bf2113",
   "metadata": {},
   "outputs": [],
   "source": [
    "'''\n",
    "While Conformal Inference is proven to be robust in many scenarios, it is not guaranteed to work everytime. Most commonly, Conformal Inference can fail when\n",
    "\n",
    "there is not enough days in the Pre-Treatment Period\n",
    "the true impact (called in the paper as Shock Sequence Ut) is not stationary or not weakly dependent\n",
    "the data is not stationary and not weakly dependent \n",
    "when our model is misspecified or inconsistent\n",
    "\n",
    "'''"
   ]
  },

  {
   "cell_type": "code",

   "metadata": {},
   "outputs": [],
   "source": []
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": null,
   "id": "f52e4f6f",
   "metadata": {},
   "outputs": [],
   "source": []
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": 167,
   "id": "75478ace",
   "metadata": {},
   "outputs": [],
   "source": [
    "from typing import Callable, Optional, Callable, Dict\n",
    "\n",
    "def resolve_end_period(time_period):\n",
    "    if time_period.end is None:\n",
    "        return None\n",
    "    else:\n",
    "        return time_period.end+1\n",
    "    \n",
    "    \n",
    "\n",
    "def jkp(panel: PanelDataset, estimator: Callable , alpha = .1 , **estimator_kwargs) -> np.array:\n",
    "     \n",
    "    pre_t_time = panel.treated_start_idxs[0]-1\n",
    "    n_treated_units = len(panel.treated_units)\n",
    "    n_treated_time_periods = (panel.treated_end_idxs[0] - panel.treated_start_idxs[0])+1\n",
    "    \n",
    "    lower_df = np.zeros((pre_t_time\n",
    "              , n_treated_time_periods\n",
    "              , n_treated_units))\n",
    "\n",
    "    upper_df = np.zeros((pre_t_time\n",
    "              , n_treated_time_periods\n",
    "              , n_treated_units))\n",
    "\n",
    "    for t_unit in range(pre_t_time):\n",
    "\n",
    "        # drop t unit from pre-treatment periods\n",
    "        new_wide_df = panel.wide_data.drop(panel.times[t_unit], axis=1)\n",
    "\n",
    "        # append to end\n",
    "        new_wide_df = new_wide_df.assign(new_column = panel.wide_data.loc[:, panel.times[t_unit]]).rename(columns={\"new_column\": panel.times[-1]+1})\n",
    "\n",
    "        # new PDS\n",
    "        new_pds = PanelDataset(new_wide_df\n",
    "                               , [TimePeriod(start=tp.start, end=resolve_end_period(tp)) for tp in panel.treated_periods]\n",
    "                               , panel.treated_units)\n",
    "        # fit new model \n",
    "        model = estimator(**estimator_kwargs)\n",
    "        model.run_analysis(new_pds)\n",
    "\n",
    "        residuals = model.results['y'][new_pds.treated_start_idxs[0]:] - model.results['y_hat'][new_pds.treated_start_idxs[0]:]\n",
    "        error = np.abs(residuals[-1])\n",
    "\n",
    "        upper = residuals[:-1] + error\n",
    "        lower = residuals[:-1] - error\n",
    "\n",
    "        lower_df[t_unit] += lower\n",
    "        upper_df[t_unit] += upper\n",
    "    \n",
    "    return np.percentile(lower_df, alpha*100, axis=0), np.percentile(upper_df, alpha*100, axis=0)\n",
    "    \n",
    "    "
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": 169,
   "id": "495d190c",
   "metadata": {},
   "outputs": [],
   "source": [
    "upper, lower = jkp(pds,TBRRidge , alpha = .1 ) "
   ]
  },

   "cell_type": "code",

    "import numpy as np\n",
    "\n",
    "residuals = model.results['y'][new_pds.treated_start_idxs[0]:] - model.results['y_hat'][new_pds.treated_start_idxs[0]:]\n",
    "\n",
    "error = np.abs(residuals[-1])\n",
    "\n",
    "upper = residuals[:-1] + error\n",
    "lower = residuals[:-1] - error"
   ]
  },
